title: Massive duplicate proxy-state-construction between start_server and start_server_with_shutdown
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

Dimension: Structural Modularity / DRY

Location: src/proxy.rs lines 1631-1649 (start_server, 14 lines) vs 1657-1814 (start_server_with_shutdown, 158 lines).

Violation: start_server_with_shutdown duplicates the ENTIRE proxy initialization pipeline (Client builder, hook merging, rules engine, compactor, session manager, plugin discovery, MCP connect loop, OAuth store, VDD engine setup, SessionStart hook) already encapsulated in build_proxy_state() and fire_session_start(). Lines 1664-1792 are copy-pasted logic that must stay in sync with build_proxy_state()/connect_mcp_servers()/fire_session_start() — any change to provisioning (e.g., adding a subsystem) has two authoritative sites. Classic Stovepipe anti-pattern. The #[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)] attribute on line 1657 is the tell — it is suppressing the warning instead of fixing the root cause. Per project memory rule, allow(dead_code)/allow(too_many_lines) is forbidden.

Mandated Refactor:
1. Delete the inline block in start_server_with_shutdown (lines 1664-1792) and replace with: let state = build_proxy_state(config).await?; fire_session_start(&state).await;
2. Remove the #[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)] attribute.
3. The graceful-shutdown watcher loop (lines 1802-1809) should be the ONLY unique logic in this function. Net result: start_server_with_shutdown becomes ~20 lines.
4. Consider removing start_server entirely and having callers use start_server_with_shutdown with a never-resolving watcher.
